scene.title: The Road to the Lists
scene.intro: {name} sends word from the road to the tourney-town, where holds and guilds send champions to fight for a purse and a season's glory. {They} ask{s} one thing before the first bout: is this to be won clean, on craft and courage — or should {they} carry a quiet working, and make certain?

advisors.text: Clean, Archon. A crown of cheating is a crown of glass. If we win, let it be ours in truth.
Everyone at those lists is cheating a little. Tell {name} to carry the working. Winning is the reputation; how, is only gossip.

choices.label: Fight clean — craft and courage only

win.text: You send back one word: honestly. {name} sets {their} jaw and rides on to the lists with nothing up {their} sleeve but skill.
win.saga: You sent {name} to the champion's tourney to fight clean, on craft and courage alone.

choices.label: Send a quiet working to make certain

win.text: You send {name} a working folded small — a whisper to slow a blade, to steady a hand. It is not honest. It may be enough.
win.saga: You sent {name} to the tourney with a quiet working hidden — victory made certain, if none should see.

scene.title: The Early Bouts
scene.intro: {name} has come through the first rounds, and sends word by fast rider. The field narrows to the sharp and the lucky, and the crowd has begun to chant a name — {theirs}.

advisors.text: {name} is fighting beautifully, Archon. Whatever we told {them} to do, tell {them} to keep doing it.

win.text: {name} wins {their} bracket on skill alone, and the honest crowd loves {them} for it. On to the final.
win.saga: {name} fought clean through the tourney's early bouts and won a place in the final on skill alone.

choices.label: Word: keep the working hidden and win

win.text: The working does its quiet work and no marshal is the wiser. {name} advances, {their} secret intact and {their} conscience less so. On to the final.
win.saga: {name} carried the hidden working through the tourney's early bouts unseen, and bought a place in the final.

lose.text: A grey-robed adjudicator feels the working's edge and calls it aloud. {name} is not disqualified — nothing is proven — but the whole field watches {them} now, and the crowd's love has curdled to suspicion.
lose.saga: {name}'s hidden working was half-caught at the tourney; nothing was proven, but suspicion followed {them} into the final.

scene.title: The Final Bout
scene.intro: The final. {name} faces the tourney's iron favourite before every hold and guild that sent a champion. Whatever Runehold did to get {them} here, it comes down to this ring, this bout, this breath.

advisors.text: One bout, Archon. Whatever {name} carries into that ring — skill or secret — let {them} spend all of it now.

win.text: {name} takes the favourite in three passes and the purse and the crown with it. Runehold's name rings around the lists — a small hearth with a champion. {They} ride{s} home in glory.
win.saga: {name} won the champion's tourney before every hold and guild, and carried the crown and purse home to Runehold.

lose.text: {name} fights well and loses well, beaten clean by a better hand in the ring. No shame in it — but no crown either. {They} come{s} home the runner-up, and a little wiser.
lose.saga: {name} reached the final of the champion's tourney and was beaten clean — runner-up, and no shame in it.
